OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Off Calendar

Defendant Think Investments Lp And Shashin Shahs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Confidential Documents
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT THINK INVESTMENTS, LP, SHASHIN SHAH's Motion To Seal Confidential Documents.

Appearances:
Thomas K. Cauley, Esq. of Sidley Austin LLP, Tel. 312.853.7076, for defendants and moving party, by Court Call, with Ryan Sandrock, Esq., present in court;
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., Tel. 415.788.1945, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. AT the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THINK INVESTMENTS, LP, SHASHIN's Motion To Seal Confidential Documents is granted as to the financial data contained in the Separation Agreements and references to it in defendants, memorandum of points and authorities and is denied as to the rest of the documents. An ordinary settlement agreement or separation agreement does not present overriding confidentiality interests except with regard to confidential financial data and Think International's concern is about harm resulting from competitors learning about how much Think international pays its employees. See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1286S. Because the financial terms contained in the Settlement Agreement between Caldwell and Think International are already part of the public record, only the financial terms of the Separation Agreement, namely the sum Think International agreed to pay Ms. Hunt should be sealed to protect defendant's confidential financial information.

Counsel for Think Investments is to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 2.550, 2.551 and 3.1312. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas K. Cauley, Esq. of Sidley Austin LLP, Tel. 312.853.7076, for defendants and moving party, by Court Call, with Ryan Sandrock, Esq., present in court;
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., Tel. 415.788.1945, for plaintiff.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. VERIFIED APPLICATION OF THOMAS K. CAULEY TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE for defendants Think Investments, LP and Shashin Shah is GRANTED. No opposition filed and the application complies with CRC 9.40. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas K. Cauley, Esq. of Sidley Austin LLP, Tel. 312.853.7076, for defendants and moving party, by Court Call, with Ryan Sandrock, Esq., present in court;
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., Tel. 415.788.1945, for plaintiff.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. VERIFIED APPLICATION OF JOSEPH R. DOSCH TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE for defendants Think Investments, LP and Shashin Shah is GRANTED. No opposition filed and the application complies with CRC 9.40. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas K. Cauley, Esq. of Sidley Austin LLP, Tel. 312.853.7076, for defendants and moving party, by Court Call, with Ryan Sandrock, Esq., present in court;
Charles J. Wisch, Esq., Tel. 415.788.1945, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS THINK INVESTMENTS, LP, SHASHIN SHAH's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The court takes judicial notice of the existence of the Settlement Agreement and the Separation Agreement but not of the truthfulness or interpretation of the statements therein since they are subject to dispute. Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113. Caldwell has alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for breach of contract, by alleging that Think Investment disclosed to Ms. Hunt that part of her compensation will be deferred because Think Investment wanted to avoid paying the full amount to Caldwell under the Settlement Agreement and that under the Separation Agreement, Think Investment resolved Hunt's claim for compensation due but payed her after June 30, 2017 in order to avoid his contractual obligation to Caldwell. The facts alleged are sufficient to support Caldwell's promissory fraud cause of action. Caldwell's allegation that Think Investment disclosed to Ms. Hunt that part of her compensation for 2016 will be deferred support the element of intent not to perform at time of contracting. While the allegations as to the Think Investments' disclosures to Ms. Hunt and Think Investments' fraudulent intent are stated on "information and belief" the facts alleged and reasonable inferences from these fact support that belief. Think Investment represented to Caldwell that it was not anticipating any additional compensation to be paid to Ms. Hunt, payed $1,188 as a final payment under the Settlement Agreement, and only two days afterwards formally entered into the Separation Agreement under which Ms. Hunt was to be paid more than $600,000. The timing and the manner of the payment to Ms. Hunt allows for a reasonable inference supporting Caldwell's allegations that the Think Investments' representation to Caldwell was fraudulent. Caldwell has alleged enough facts that support a fraudulent concealment cause of action where the duty to disclose arises from the settlement agreement.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.
